questions.text: Who is the owner / DRI (Directly Responsible Individual) for this PRD?
questions.context: The owner field is empty. Accountability is required for spec-format compliance and for escalation paths during implementation.

questions.text: Are there any performance or availability targets for Phase 1? For example: 'API endpoints must respond within 500 ms at p99', 'the service must tolerate X requests/second', or 'no formal SLO for Phase 1 — best effort only'.
questions.context: Without at least a minimal performance expectation, QA cannot define load tests and infrastructure sizing is unconstrained.
options: No formal SLO for Phase 1 — best effort only
p99 latency < 500 ms for all API endpoints under normal load

questions.text: What API versioning strategy should be used? Should all Phase 1 endpoints be prefixed with /v1/, use a header-based version, or have no versioning scheme yet?
questions.context: An agreed versioning strategy prevents breaking changes when Phase 2 endpoints are added and ensures CLI and AF-instance clients can target a stable surface.
options: No versioning in Phase 1 — defer to a future phase

questions.text: What should the standard error response body look like? For example: { "error": "string", "code": "string" } or simply an HTTP status code with no body?
questions.context: A consistent error format is needed for CLI error handling, AF-instance retry logic, and future API consumers. Defining it now avoids divergence across endpoints.
options: HTTP status code only, no body

answers.q10: no SLOs or SLAs for Phase 1
answers.q12: HTTP status code and { "error": "<human-readable message>", "code": "<machine-readable code>" } body
